methods.name: Stage-Progression

data.narrative:
Saronic is a Series D-stage defense and security company currently valued at $9.25B post-money, having raised $2.6B+ to date. The peer universe consists of two public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and eight private defense-tech companies at similar or adjacent stages, including Chaos Industries, Epirus, Onebrief, Anduril Industries, and Flock Safety.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $9.2B last post-money valuation and applying a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical of Series D advancement, yields $13.9B. Peer-median analysis across three same-sector, same-stage comparables—Chaos Industries ($4.5B), Epirus ($1.4B), and Onebrief ($1.1B)—produces a median of $1.4B, with a range of $1.1B to $4.5B. The divergence between these methods reflects material variation in peer valuations within the defense sector.

Triangulating both approaches yields a range of $1.1B (low) to $16.0B (high), with a median of $7.6B. Saronic's current $9.25B valuation sits above the median but within the upper quartile of this range, consistent with stage-progression logic. Key uncertainties include the comparability of the three peer companies to Saronic's specific technology and market position, and the applicability of the 1.5x multiplier to this higher-valuation company within a nascent, heterogeneous sector.
